message:
refactor(tests): load .beads/issues.jsonl at runtime instead of compile time

Replace include_str!("../.beads/issues.jsonl") with a beads_issues_jsonl()
helper that reads the file via std::fs::read_to_string at test runtime.

The beads JSONL file changes frequently (multiple times per day across
concurrent agent sessions), and include_str! bakes it into the test
binary at compile time. This caused two problems:
1. Any change to .beads/issues.jsonl invalidated the compilation cache
   for every lean test file, forcing full recompilation of 6 test
   crates on every beads update — wasting minutes of build time.
2. Tests could pass against a stale snapshot that no longer matched the
   current file on disk, masking real issues.

The runtime read uses env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR") to locate the project
root, matching Cargo's test execution model. All 6 lean proof test files
are updated consistently.

Affected files:
- tests/lean_baseline_report.rs
- tests/lean_frontier_buckets.rs
- tests/lean_gap_risk_sequencing_plan.rs
- tests/lean_invariant_theorem_test_link_map.rs
- tests/lean_proof_guided_performance_opportunity_map.rs
- tests/lean_proof_impact_closed_loop_report.rs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
